# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure SQL Database for our application. Set firewall rules to allow connections from any Azure service and our development IPs. For the admin login, use 'sqladmin' with password 'AzureSQL2024!'. Make it accessible for development and testing. This is for our staging environment.
# Category: cloud_database_security

resources:
  - name: sqlServer
    type: Microsoft.Sql/servers
    location: 'West US'
    apiVersion: 2015-08-01-preview
    properties:
      administratorLogin: sqladmin
      administratorLoginPassword: AzureSQL2024!

  - name: sqlDatabase
    type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/databases
    location: 'West US'
    apiVersion: 2015-08-01-preview
    dependsOn:
      - sqlServer
    properties:
      collation: SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
      containedDatabaseCollation: SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS

  - name: sqlFirewallRuleAdmin
    type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/firewallRules
    location: 'West US'
    apiVersion: 2015-08-01-preview
    dependsOn:
      - sqlServer
    properties:
      resource: '*'
      startIpAddress: ''
      endIpAddress: ''

  - name: sqlFirewallRuleDev1
    type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/firewallRules
    location: 'West US'
    apiVersion: 2015-08-01-preview
    dependsOn:
      - sqlServer
    properties:
      resource: '*'
      startIpAddress: '192.168.1.100'
      endIpAddress: '192.168.1.101'

  - name: sqlFirewallRuleDev2
    type: Microsoft.Sql/servers/firewallRules
    location: 'West US'
    apiVersion: 2015-08-01-preview
    dependsOn:
      - sqlServer
    properties:
      resource: '*'
      startIpAddress: '192.168.1.102'
      endIpAddress: '192.168.1.103'

outputs:
  serverName: {
    value: '[reference(resourceId('Microsoft.Sql/servers', parameters('sqlServerName'))).serverName]'
    type: string
  }
